chapters.content_preview: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook  1. The 27 Nakshatras and Their Ruling Lords  In Vedic astrology, the zodiac is divided into 27 lunar mansions or constellations known as Nakshatras. chapters.title: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook
chapters.content_preview: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook  2. Tithis, Their Nature, and Dagdha Rashi  A Tithi is a lunar day. chapters.content_preview: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook  3. Auspicious (Shubh) and Inauspicious (Ashubh) Yogas  Yogas are specific combinations of Weekdays (Vara), Lunar Days (Tithis), or Nakshatras that produce highly auspicious or inauspicious periods for Muhurat (timing). chapters.title: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook
chapters.content_preview: Vedic Astrology Quick Reference Handbook  5. Do Ghati Muhurat (Time Cycles)  A Ghati is approx. 24 minutes. 'Do Ghati' means two Ghatis, making a 48-minute block. The day consists of 30 Do Ghati Muhurats. Each is linked to a specific Nakshatra/Deity.  Seq Muhurat/Deity Nakshatra
